senses.definition: from a time that has begun a long ago

examples.x: They have celebrated this festival from the year one, passing down the traditions through generations.
The organization continues to thrive, maintaining its commitment to excellence since the year one.
He has been an active member of the club since the year one, contributing to its growth and success.
The village had celebrated their annual harvest festival since the year one, creating cherished memories for all.
The company flourished for decades, providing quality products from the year one.
The company has been operating since year one, establishing itself as a leader in the industry.

senses.definition: at a point in time as a process or action continues or is being carried out

examples.x: We'll have to address that issue down the road when it becomes more critical.
He plans to buy a bigger house down the road when he saves enough money.
Down the road, I hope to travel to exotic destinations and explore different cultures.
We'll discuss potential promotions and career growth opportunities down the road.
Let's focus on building a strong foundation now, so we can reap the benefits down the road.
She dreams of starting her own business down the road and being her own boss.

senses.definition: a work day that is short compared to total hours of most jobs

examples.x: Our company has adjusted its schedule to accommodate bankers' hours during the pandemic.
The business struggled because it didn't align with customers' needs and operated on bankers' hours.
The new branch of the bank plans to extend its hours, moving away from traditional bankers' hours to better serve customers.

word_list.pronunciation: ɪn ɔːɹ ˌoʊvɚ ðə lˈɑːŋ ɹˈʌn

senses.definition: in the end of or over a long period of time

examples.x: Learning a second language can open up more career opportunities in the long run.
Implementing sustainable practices in businesses can lead to significant environmental benefits in the long run.
Over the long run, technological advancements have revolutionized industries and transformed the way we live.
By consistently saving and investing, individuals can accumulate significant wealth over the long run.
In the long run, regular exercise will improve your health.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈɪl ʌntˈɪl ðə kˈaʊz kˈʌm hˈoʊm

senses.definition: used to describe a prolonged or indefinite period of time, often implying that something will continue for a very long duration

examples.x: They will talk about their favorite TV show till the cows come home if you let them.
She will keep practicing her instrument till the cows come home to perfect her skills.
They are going to party till the cows come home tonight.
He will be studying till the cows come home for the upcoming exams.
We have been waiting for the results until the cows come home, and they still haven't arrived.

senses.definition: to last only for a short period of time

examples.x: Friends come and go, but true friendships can stand the test of time.
Opportunities in life often come and go, so it's important to seize them when they arise.
Emotions can come and go like waves, sometimes intense and other times fleeting.
Success in the entertainment industry can be unpredictable, with fame and popularity coming and going rapidly.

senses.definition: used to refer to a long passage of time or a significant number of months or years

examples.x: I haven't seen her in many moons; it feels like ages since our last meeting.
Many moons from now, people will look back on these days as a significant era.
She was traveling the world many moons ago, exploring different cultures.
They had been neighbors for many moons before finally becoming close friends.
He had been dreaming of his wedding day for many moons, and now it was finally approaching.

word_list.pronunciation: sˈʌmwɛɹ ɔːɹ ɐlˈɑːŋ ɔːɹ dˌaʊn ðə lˈaɪn

senses.definition: at an unspecified point during a series of events or a process

examples.x: Somewhere along the line, we lost track of the project's original goals.
She used to be a great student, but somewhere along the line, her grades started slipping.
Along the line, he discovered his passion for photography.
Her dedication to her job wavered along the line.
We may encounter difficulties down the line.
Despite our preparations, unforeseen obstacles may arise somewhere down the line, complicating our project timeline.

senses.definition: a very prolonged period of time

examples.x: It feels like a month of Sundays since I last had a day off.
The project is taking so long that it seems like a month of Sundays.
She was waiting in line for what felt like a month of Sundays.
By the time the renovations are complete, it will have been a month of Sundays.
By the time I finally received a response, it had been a month of Sundays since I sent the email.

senses.definition: used to say that something has existed or been done for an extremely long time, to the point where its origin or beginning is unknown or forgotten

examples.x: People have been practicing this traditional dance since time immemorial.
The ancient temple had been a sacred site from time immemorial.
They will continue to celebrate this festival as they have done since time immemorial.
By this time next year, they will have been preserving their cultural heritage since time immemorial.
By the time the archaeologists arrived, the ruins had stood there from time immemorial.
